code: library(grf) library(UtopiaPlanitia)  set.seed(1995) n <- 500; p <- 5 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p) colnames(X) <- paste0("X", 1:p) W <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5) Y <- X[, 1] * W + rnorm(n)  cf <- causal_forest(X, Y, W)  # S3 summary: ATE, variable importance, heterogeneity tests summary(cf)  # S3 plot: diagnostics (default), or type = "rank", "pdp", "inter" plot(cf, type = "rank")  # LOCO variable importance vi <- cf_loco(cf) summary(vi) plot(vi)

title: Auto-Selected Nuisance Causal Forest — autocf
text: Refits grf::causal_forest() replacing conditional outcome m(x) = E[Y | X = x] propensity e(x) = E[W | X = x] best-performing nuisance estimator candidate pool, scored shared K-fold cross-fit weighted CV loss. CATE estimator identification assumptions grf unchanged — Robinson-residualisation nuisances swapped (winner beats grf's baseline configurable margin).

text: c.forest fitted causal forest object grf package, used recover (X, Y, W) (via c.forest$X.orig, c.forest$Y.orig, c.forest$W.orig) inherit tunable hyperparameters refit. X, Y, W Optional overrides design matrix, outcome vector, treatment vector. NULL (default) recovered c.forest. X must fully numeric: grf baseline (always present) final grf::causal_forest() refit require numeric design matrix, factor/character columns rejected — encode (e.g. one-hot via model.matrix()) fitting. NAs X tolerated: grf, xgboost, bart, tabpfn handle missingness natively; glmnet candidate auto-imputes via glmnet::makeX(na.impute = TRUE) (mean imputation), fit per training fold applied held-fold avoid leakage. NAs Y W allowed. K Integer >= 2. Number cross-fit folds shared across candidates. Default 5. seed Integer seed fold assignment, per-candidate RNG, downstream grf::causal_forest() call. Default 1995. eps Numeric (0, 0.5). Propensity clipping bound binary W (winner's predictions clipped [eps, 1 - eps] preserve overlap). Default 1e-3. tuning Character scalar controlling grf::causal_forest() tunable hyperparameters handled refit. One "orig" (default, inherit verbatim c.forest$tunable.params), "cf.default", "cf.autotune". User ... overrides win. pool Character vector candidate nuisance estimators compare. Default c("grf", "glmnet", "xgboost", "tabpfn", "bart"). candidate whose required dependency missing (incompatible non-trivial sample.weights) dropped warning. "grf" baseline always refit K-fold cross-fit protocol candidates (using grf::regression_forest() held-fold), CV losses apples--apples. "bart" candidate uses dbarts::bart() dbarts package defaults, parallel chains via dbarts::guessNumCores() deterministic seed. min_improvement Either character string "1se" (default), character string "0", non-negative numeric. Controls margin candidate must beat grf baseline trigger nuisance swap. "1se" requires mean fold-wise loss reduction exceed paired standard error fold differences (1-SE rule). "0" swaps improvement. numeric value swaps absolute loss reduction exceeds . term_evals Integer >= 0. Random-search budget xgboost AutoTuner (per outer fold per nuisance). 0 disables tuning uses mlr3's default xgboost hyperparameters. Default 10L. tabpfn_args, glmnet_args, xgboost_args, bart_args Optional named lists per-candidate extra arguments. tabpfn_args forwarded tabpfn::tab_pfn(); glmnet_args glmnet::cv.glmnet(); xgboost_args forwarded mlr3 xgboost learner's param_set$values (use set, e.g., nthread = 4 multi-core CPU device = "cuda" enable GPU systems CUDA xgboost build; defaults mlr3's reproducibility-oriented nthread = 1 CPU). bart_args forwarded dbarts::bart(); default adapter sets nchain nthread dbarts::guessNumCores() uses dbarts defaults everything else (ntree = 200, ndpost = 1000, nskip = 100, keeptrees = FALSE); user keys bart_args override . Tuned hyperparameters override matching keys. default list(). verbose Logical. Print per-candidate / per-fold progress. Default FALSE. ... title: Auto-Selected Nuisance Causal Forest — autocf
text: function . diagnostic + drop-replacement. grf's default nuisance estimators (regression forests Y W) usually fine always best. autocf() runs small candidate pool shared K-fold cross-fit protocol weighted CV loss (mean-squared error Y continuous W; Brier score binary W, identical MSE 0/1 outcomes), reports comparison table, swaps competitor beats grf baseline margin choose. grf baseline scored --bag. "grf" candidate scored fitted forest's --bag honest nuisances (c.forest$Y.hat / c.forest$W.hat) — predictions actually keep — rather refit per fold. Every candidate still scored strict K-fold OOF predictions. Per-fold weighted MSE grf baseline computed binning OOB residuals K folds, 1-SE comparison machinery unchanged. 1-SE rule swap. finite-sample CV noise non-trivial candidate produce random improvement particular seed. Default min_improvement = "1se" requires mean fold-wise loss reduction exceed paired-fold standard error reduction: $$\text{swap} \iff   \bar d_K - \widehat{SE}(\bar d_K) > 0,   \quad d_k = \text{loss}^{\text{grf}}_k - \text{loss}^{\text{cand}}_k.$$ kind conservativism glmnet's lambda.1se uses, generalized model selection. Use min_improvement = "0" force improvement fire, supply numeric absolute threshold. Weighted CV losses. c.forest$sample.weights non-trivial, training per-fold loss computation weighted, loss minimised matches chosen nuisances actually used downstream grf. Candidates accept training weights (currently tabpfn) dropped pool warning weights non-trivial. Selection-induced inference. Picking best M candidates CV loss model-selection step. benign grf::causal_forest() uses Neyman-orthogonal R-learner score second stage; small perturbations chosen nuisance invalidate sqrt(n) inference (Chernozhukov et al., 2018; Foster & Syrgkanis, 2019). Without orthogonality safe. Convergence-rate caveat. CV-loss minimization picks lowest finite-sample loss, best convergence rate. valid sqrt(n) downstream inference (e.g. grf::average_treatment_effect()), chosen nuisances must converge o(n^{-1/4}). default pool members (grf reg-forest, glmnet sparsity, xgboost boosting consistency, BART Rockova & van der Pas, 2020) believed adequate typical n; tabpfn formal rate proof treated exploratory downstream confidence intervals matter. Reproducibility future plans. xgboost's mlr3 AutoTuner inner CV runs future framework. prevent non-deterministic seeding user-set future::plan(multisession) similar, autocf() forces future::plan("sequential") duration call restores previous plan exit.

title: Auto-Selected Nuisance Causal Forest — autocf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(grf) set.seed(1995) n <- 400; p <- 5 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p) colnames(X) <- paste0("X", seq_len(p)) W <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5) Y <- X[, 1] * W + rnorm(n) cf <- causal_forest(X, Y, W, num.trees = 200)  # Default: compare grf, glmnet, xgboost, tabpfn, bart (whatever is installed) cf2 <- autocf(cf, K = 5) attr(cf2, "autocf_meta")$scores attr(cf2, "autocf_meta")$winner_y attr(cf2, "autocf_meta")$winner_w  # Smaller pool, force any improvement to swap cf3 <- autocf(cf, pool = c("grf", "glmnet"), min_improvement = "0") } # }

title: Modified LOCO Variable Importance for Causal Forests — cf_loco
text: LOCO measures. Leave-one-covariate-(LOCO) importance drops covariate (group covariates) model, refits forest without , measures much worse individualized predictions become. large score means variable important predicting heterogeneous treatment effects; score near zero means forest can recover CATEs without variable. Debiasing. Naive LOCO biased refitting forest introduces retrain variance. implementation uses debiased estimator Benard Josse (2023): full-data refit used bias correction term, per-observation CATE re-estimation performed via forest weights rather raw OOB predictions. Computational cost. function refits p + 1 causal forests (one per dropped variable plus one full-data refit), hyperparameters original. large datasets many covariates, consider using screen argument reduce number refits. Normalization. normalize = TRUE, negative importance scores (can occur dropping variable improves predictions) set zero, remaining scores rescaled sum one. gives proportional importance interpretation discards directional information. Variable grouping. Highly correlated covariates can grouped dropped together. Use group..corr = TRUE automatic grouping via correlation threshold, supply manual list via variable.groups.

code: # \donttest{ library(grf) set.seed(1995) n <- 200; p <- 5 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p) colnames(X) <- paste0("X", seq_len(p)) W <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5) Y <- X[, 1] * W + rnorm(n) cf <- causal_forest(X, Y, W, num.trees = 100) vi <- cf_loco(cf) summary(vi) #> LOCO Variable Importance (Benard and Josse, 2023) #>   n = 200  p = 5  #>   Normalized: FALSE  #>  #>  Variable Importance #>        X1   0.976803 #>        X2  -0.008422 #>        X3  -0.008641 #>        X5  -0.012525 #>        X4  -0.013253 plot(vi)   # Normalized importance (sums to 1) vi_norm <- cf_loco(cf, normalize = TRUE) summary(vi_norm) #> LOCO Variable Importance (Benard and Josse, 2023) #>   n = 200  p = 5  #>   Normalized: TRUE  #>  #>  Variable Importance #>        X1          1 #>        X2          0 #>        X3          0 #>        X4          0 #>        X5          0 # }

title: Causal Forest with cv.glmnet-Estimated Nuisances — glmcf
text: changes vs. grf::causal_forest()? nuisance predictions used centre Y W. CATE estimator, honesty splits, identification assumptions inherited grf. cv.glmnet? Penalized regression fast, well-understood baseline nuisance estimation Robinson-style residualisation (Chernozhukov et al., 2018, treat lasso/elastic-net nuisances canonical DML first stage). Linearity--X assumption (load-bearing). glmcf() fits cv.glmnet X supplied, Y.hat W.hat linear columns X (elastic-net penalty + optional relax). true m(x) = E[Y | X] e(x) = E[W | X] non-linear X, lasso residuals carry residual confounding correlated X, biases Robinson centering propagates CATE estimates downstream. grf's residual--residual splits absorb . non-linear nuisances, pre-transform X (interactions, splines, polynomials) passing glmcf(), use tabcf() handles non-linearity natively. DML rate requirement downstream inference. valid sqrt(n) inference grf::average_treatment_effect() (similar), DML theory requires nuisances converge o(n^{-1/4}). Lasso achieves s_n * sqrt(log(p) / n) approximate sparsity (effective sparsity s_n, ambient dimension p). small p sparse signal satisfied; moderate p (e.g. p > sqrt(n)) non-sparse signal , ATE confidence intervals grf -cover. wide-X / non-sparse regime, prefer tabcf() random-forest nuisances. Cross-fitting. Predictions produced via K-fold cross-fitting: fold, cv.glmnet() fit K - 1 folds predicts held-fold. c.forest$clusters non-trivial, folds built units cluster end fold (preserving grf's cluster-aware inference). Treatment type. W treated binary {0, 1}-valued (numeric integer levels present), logical, 2-level factor; case family = "binomial" used predictions clipped [eps, 1 - eps] preserve overlap. Otherwise W treated continuous family = "gaussian" used. W numeric exactly 2 unique values {0, 1} (e.g. {1, 2} {-1, 1}), glmcf() emits warning falls back gaussian regressor; encode treatments 0/1 use classifier. Alpha tuning reproducibility. Within cross-fit fold, inner CV run single shared foldid across candidate alphas minimum-CV-deviance comparison across alphas splits. selected alpha may differ across folds Y W. Per-fold reselection consistent rate data-dependent: small n_train, differences CV deviance across alphas within sampling error, selected alpha can vary resampling (actual selections recorded attr(, "glmcf_meta")$alpha_y $alpha_w diagnostics). studies need reproducible CIs resampling (bootstrap, Monte Carlo), prefer single fixed alpha (e.g. alpha = 1 pure lasso) default grid. Sample weights. c.forest$sample.weights passed cv.glmnet() via weights argument weighted training consistent nuisance fits downstream causal forest. Dependencies. glmnet package gated Suggests dependency. glmcf() calls rlang::check_installed("glmnet") top raises informative error missing.

title: LOCO Variable Importance for Outcome Models — loco
text: model fitted outcome forest. Supported classes: ranger::ranger() (regression, probability, classification), grf::regression_forest(), grf::boosted_regression_forest(), grf::probability_forest(). Survival, causal, quantile, instrumental, multi-arm, lm_forest objects rejected. data Optional. data frame containing variables used fit model. meaningful ranger models. code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("ranger", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(1995)   dat <- data.frame(y = rnorm(100), x1 = rnorm(100), x2 = rnorm(100),                     x3 = rnorm(100))   mod <- ranger::ranger(y ~ ., data = dat, num.trees = 50)   loco(mod, split = FALSE)   loco(mod, split = FALSE,        groups = list(g1 = c("x1","x2"), g2 = "x3"))   set.seed(2026)   dat2 <- data.frame(y = factor(sample(0:1, 100, replace = TRUE)),                      x1 = rnorm(100), x2 = rnorm(100), x3 = rnorm(100))   mod_prob <- ranger::ranger(y ~ ., data = dat2, num.trees = 50,                              probability = TRUE)   loco(mod_prob, split = FALSE)            # auto -> "brier"   loco(mod_prob, split = FALSE, loss = "log") } #>   variable importance method loss #> 1       x2 0.09788476    oob  log #> 2       x3 0.07353345    oob  log #> 3       x1 0.02890264    oob  log if (requireNamespace("grf", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(1995)   X <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 3), 100, 3)   colnames(X) <- c("x1", "x2", "x3")   Y <- X[, 1] + 0.5 * X[, 2] + rnorm(100, sd = 0.5)   rf <- grf::regression_forest(X, Y, num.trees = 100)   loco(rf, split = FALSE)                  # auto -> "abs"   Yf <- factor(rbinom(100, 1, plogis(X[, 1])))   pf <- grf::probability_forest(X, Yf, num.trees = 100)   loco(pf, split = FALSE)                  # auto -> "brier" } #>   variable  importance method  loss #> 1       x1 0.063156781    oob brier #> 2       x2 0.005360043    oob brier #> 3       x3 0.005281682    oob brier # }

title: Omnibus Tests of Heterogeneity — omni_hetero
text: function combines five tests treatment effect heterogeneity, grouped Preferred (valid size) Heuristic categories: Preferred tests (valid Type error control): Sequential RATE (Wager, 2024): k-fold cross-validated test correct size (valid Type error). trustworthy test battery preferred formal inference. Calibration test (Chernozhukov et al., 2018): Regresses doubly robust scores forest's CATE predictions. "differential forest prediction" coefficient tests whether forest captures meaningful heterogeneity beyond ATE. Heuristic tests (useful screening, formal inference): High vs. low CATE, cross-fit (Athey Wager, 2019): Splits sample half, predicts CATEs half using forest trained half, median-splits compares \(ATE_{high}\) vs. \(ATE_{low}\) within held-half. Cross-fitting avoids winner's curse contaminates naive -data version (see grf PR #1502). OOB RATE, two-sided (heuristic): Uses --bag CATE predictions directly. Known anti-conservative (~30\ null nominal 5\ screening tool, formal inference. OOB RATE, one-sided (heuristic): One-sided version . Approximately valid direction heterogeneity pre-specified.

what: tabcf() gains repeated cross-fitting and clip control
title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: New R argument repeated K-fold cross-fitting. Nuisance predictions averaged R independent fold partitions cut Monte-Carlo variance. R = 1 (default) identical previous behavior. New clip argument controls binary-propensity clipping: FALSE (default; clipping, overlap warning), TRUE (clip [1e-3, 1 - 1e-3]), c(lo, hi) custom bounds. Behavior change: binary propensities longer clipped default. Previous versions always clipped 1e-3; default now warn-. Pass clip = TRUE restore old clipping. eps deprecated favor clip. maps clip = c(eps, 1 - eps) deprecation warning.

title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: loco() now accepts grf outcome forests addition ranger. Supported classes: grf::regression_forest(), grf::boosted_regression_forest(), grf::probability_forest(). Causal, survival, quantile, instrumental, multi-arm, lm_forest objects rejected informative error messages; causal_forest() error points users cf_loco(). Backend auto-detected model class — new argument behavior change existing ranger users. Hyperparameters replayed fitted grf object (tunable.params plus num.trees, ci.group.size, clusters, equalize.cluster.weights). grf::boosted_regression_forest(), boost.steps replayed length(model$forests); boost.error.reduction reverts grf’s default. sample.weights honesty flags preserved. data = ... ignored grf models one-shot warning; grf forests store training data internally. Split-mode grf models always uses internal custom split loop (conformalInference::loco() fast path remains ranger-). New tests tests/testthat/test-loco-grf.R.

what: loco() gains classification DV support and group-LOCO
title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)
text: Classification / probability forests now first-class. treetype guard relaxed: regression, probability estimation, (hard) classification ranger forests supported. Survival forests remain rejected. New loss argument ("auto", "abs", "mse", "brier", "zero_one", "log"). Defaults "auto" resolves "abs" regression (back-compat historic conformal-LOCO convention), "brier" probability forests, "zero_one" classification forests. Disallowed combinations (e.g. "brier" hard-classification forest) raise informative error pointing user refit probability = TRUE. New groups argument group-LOCO. Pass NULL (default) per-variable behavior; pass character vector single unnamed group; pass named list character vectors multiple groups dropped jointly. Validates non-overlap, known names, non-emptiness, least one predictor remains drop. New members column appears output groups supplied; list-column character vectors naming group’s members. New loss column added unconditionally output downstream consumers can tell residual flavor produced row. Split mode dispatch: groups = NULL model regression forest loss = "abs", still delegate [conformalInference::loco()] (bit--bit back-compat prior split-mode results). Every split-mode configuration (classification, probability, custom loss, group LOCO) handled new -package custom split loop mirrors conformalInference’s sample-splitting + one-sided Z Wilcoxon inference drops sets columns computes user-defined per-observation loss residuals.

title: UtopiaPlanitia (development version)

Previously loco() parsed model$call tried evaluate symbols parent.frame(), crashed model fit inside function hyperparameters bound local variables (common pattern). Core hyperparameters (num.trees, mtry, min.node.size, splitrule, replace, max.depth) now read directly model. Uncommon arguments still come call graceful warning fallback can’t resolved. OOB mode longer crashes training frame columns beyond predictors response. now refits via ranger’s x / y interface ignores extra columns. OOB mode now honors seed argument. Previously silently ignored, OOB importance non-reproducible across calls. original .Random.seed restored exit. Regression-guard. Classification, probability, survival forests now rejected informative error rather silently returning meaningless residual differences. Single-predictor models rejected early clear message instead crashing inside ranger. Factor predictors: allowed OOB mode (ranger handles natively); rejected split mode clear message conformalInference::loco() requires numeric matrix. x / y interface guard. Models fit ranger(x = X, y = y) dependent.variable.name previously silently failed deep inside train.data[[""]]. now error early workaround pointer.
